theory.intro: Two rectangular prisms can have the same volume even though their lengths, widths and heights are different. What matters is that the product length $\times$ width $\times$ height is the same.
facts: Different dimensions can give the **same product**, so the same volume.
Rearranging the same three dimensions (e.g. $2 \times 3 \times 4$) gives the same volume.
steps: Work out each prism's volume by multiplying its three dimensions.
Compare the products to decide if the volumes are equal.
To match a volume, find three numbers whose product equals it.
